*** 1 FATAL, 1 INJ *** LSR TIME/LOCATION IS FOR START POINT OF TORNADO AND ONLY COVERS WALTHALL CO SEGMENT. TOUCHED DOWN ON JEFFERSON RD. MOVED NORTHEAST THROUGH HOPE AREA THEN CROSSED HWY 27 AND CONTINUED NORTHEAST INTO LAWRENCE CO. TORNADO WAS RESPONSIBLE FOR ONE FATALITY IN WALTHALL CO AND ONE KNOWN SRIOUS INJURY. EST MAX WINDS 130 MPH. PATH LENGTH 6.67 MILES. EST PATH WIDTH 1200YD/0.67MI. INFO STILL PRELIMINARY. DETAILED PNS WILL BE ISSUED TOMORROW AFTER ALL SURVEY POINTS HAVE UNDERGONE QC.

*** 1 FATAL, 1 INJ *** TOUCHED DOWN ON JEFFERSON RD. MOVED NE THROUGH HOPE AREA THEN CROSSED HWY 27 AND CONTINUED NE INTO LAWRENCE CO. ADDITIONAL ANALYSIS OF DAMAGE INCLUDING NEW DATA POINTS SUPPORT RAISING THE MAXIMUM ESTIMATED WINDS TO 150 MPH IN THE AREA OF JAMES RATCLIFF RD. TWO FRAME HOMES WERE LEVELED IN THIS AREA. TORNADO WAS RESPONSIBLE FOR ONE FATALITY IN WALTHALL CO AND ONE KNOWN SERIOUS INJURY. EST MAX WINDS 150 MPH. PATH LENGTH 6.67 MILES. EST PATH WIDTH 1200 YARDS/0.67 MI. INFO PRELIMINARY.

*** 2 FATAL *** TOUCHED DOWN ON JEFFERSON RD. MOVED NE THROUGH HOPE AREA THEN CROSSED HWY 27 AND CONTINUED NE INTO LAWRENCE CO. ADDITIONAL ANALYSIS OF DAMAGE INCLUDING NEW DATA POINTS SUPPORT RAISING THE MAXIMUM ESTIMATED WINDS TO 150 MPH IN THE AREA OF JAMES RATCLIFF RD. TWO FRAME HOMES WERE LEVELED IN THIS AREA. TORNADO WAS RESPONSIBLE FOR ONE FATALITY IN WALTHALL CO AND ONE KNOWN SERIOUS INJURY. EST MAX WINDS 150 MPH. PATH LENGTH 6.67 MILES. EST PATH WIDTH 1200 YARDS/0.67 MI. INFO PRELIMINARY.

*** 1 FATAL, 1 INJ *** LSR TIME/LOCATION IS FOR START POINT OF TORNADO. LSR COVERS WALTHALL CO. SEGMENT. TOUCHED DOWN ON JEFFERSON RD. MOVED NE CROSSING HWY 27 AND CONTINUED NE ALNG E SARTINVILLE RD EVENTUALLY CROSSING INTO LAWRENCE CO. SUBJECT MATTER EXPERT ANALYSIS OF DAMAGE ALONG JAMES RATCLIFF RD SUPPORTS INCREASING MAX INTENSITY TO 170 MPH BASED ON ONE HOME THAT WAS REDUCED TO A SLAB. A 2ND HOME JUST TO THE NORTH SUSTAINED HIGH END EF3 DAMAGE. TORNADO WAS RESPONSIBLE FOR ONE FATALITY AND ONE KNOWN SERIOUS INJURY. PATH LENGTH 6.7 MILES. EST EST PATH WIDTH 1300 YARDS/0.75 MI.

SUBJECT MATTER EXPERT ANALYSIS OF DAMAGE ON JAMES RATCLIFF RD SUPPORTS MAX INTENSITY OF 170 MPH BASED ON ONE HOME REDUCED TO A SLAB. A 2ND HOME JUST TO THE NORTH SUSTAINED HIGH END EF3 DAMAGE. LSR TIME/LOCATION IS FOR START POINT OF TORNADO. TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN A FIELD JUST WEST OF REAGAN ROAD AND THEN MOVED EAST-NORTHEAST ALONG HWY 48 BEFORE EXITING THE COUNTY NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF MT. BETHEL ROAD AND HWY 48. SIGNIFICANT TREE DAMAGE AND MINOR DAMAGE TO A FEW HOMES WAS OBSERVED ALONG THE PATH OF THE TORNADO. TORNADO HAS BEEN RATED AN EF1 WITH MAX INTENSITY OF 110 MPH. PATH LENGTH OF 2.6 MILES. PATH WIDTH OF 500 YARDS.

UPDATED LSR TO REFLECT NWS SURVEY OF CONFIRMED TORNADO. LSR TIME/LOCATION IS FOR START POINT OF TORANDO. TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ALONG I-59 WITH SOFTWOOD TREE DAMAGE. THE TORNADO CONTINUED SOUTHEAST CROSSING MCNEIL STEEPHOLLOW ROAD WITH MULTIPLE SNAPPED TREES AND MINOR STRUCTUAL DAMAGE TO A CHICKEN COOP ON ELMER LEE ROAD. THE TORNADO ENDED TO THE SOUTHEAST CROSSING BOUIE ROAD WITH MINOR STRUCTUAL DAMAGE TO A HOME. TORNADO RATED EF1. PEAK WINDS 105MPH. PATH LENGTH 3.31 MILES. PATH WIDTH OF 200 YARDS
MULTIPLE TREES DOWN ON MCNEIL SLEEPHOLLOW RD. POSSIBLE TORNADO. RELAYED VIA SOCIAL MEDIA.

NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND DAMAGE NEAR ERWINVILLE CONSISTENT WITH EF-0 DAMAGE. THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST WEST OF BUECHE RD, TEARING SOME SIDING OFF OF A HOUSE. IN ADDITION, THE ROOF OF A GARAGE BUILDING WASDAMAGED FROM TREES. IT ALSO UPROOTED TREES AND SNAPPED TREE LIMBS AS IT TRAVERSED EASTWARD. IT LIFTED ON BUECHE RD JUST SHORT OF CLAUSE BAYOU. IT IS POSSIBLE THAT THE TORNADO STARTED AND ENDED BEFORE AND AFTER THE STATED LOCATIONS, HOWEVER THESE AREAS WERE INACCESSIBLE OR OVER FIELDS.

STORM SURVEY FOUND DAMAGE CONSISTENT WITH HIGH END EF-1, WITH ESTIMATED WINDS 110 MPH. PATH LENGTH 3.84 MILES, WIDTH 100 YARDS. MOST SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE WAS IN THE VICINITY OF AMMON STAFFING BUILDINGS ALONG 1-10 WHERE THERE WAS PARTIAL ROOF COLLAPSE. A VEHICLE WAS LIFTED AND TOSSED ONTO THE INTERSTATE. SOME MINOR DAMAGE TO WESTMINSTER ELEMENTARY SCHOOL. TORNADO LIFTED NEAR OLD HAMMOND HWY JUST EAST OF SHERWOOD BLVD.

STORM SURVEY FOUND DAMAGE CONSISTENT WITH HIGH END EF-1, WITH ESTIMATED WINDS 110 MPH. PATH LENGTH 3.5 MILES, WIDTH 100 YARDS. SOME MINOR DAMAGE TO WESTMINSTER ELEMENTARY SCHOOL. TORNADO LIFTED NEAR OLD HAMMOND HWY JUST EAST OF SHERWOOD BLVD.
POSSIBLE TORNADO. TREES, FENCES, AND POWER LINES BLOWN DOWN ON CHICKASAW AVENUE NORTH OF GREENWELL SPRING.

PRELIMINARY RATING EF-0, MAX WINDS 75 MPH. TWO TREES BLOWN DOWN ONTO VEHICLES, LARGE TREE LIMBS BLOWN ONTO A HOME. TWO FENCES AND TWO AWNINGS BLOWN DOWN. ALL DAMAGE ON CHICKASAW AVENUE NORTH OF GREENWELL SPRINGS.

PRELIMINARY RATING EF-0, MAX WINDS 75 MPH. TWO TREES BLOWN DOWN ONTO VEHICLES, LARGE TREE LIMBS BLOWN ONTO A HOME. TWO FENCES AND TWO AWNINGS BLOWN DOWN. ALL DAMAGE ON CHICKASAW AVENUE NORTH OF GREENWOOD SPRINGS.

PRELIMINARY RATING EF-1, MAX WINDS 90 MPH. THIS TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR YATASI AVE, SNAPPING AND UPROOTING TREES. TREES LANDED AND DAMAGED 2 CARS. IT THEN PROGRESSED IN A NORTHEAST DIRECTION, WHERE IT DAMAGED THE CARPORT AND FASCIA OF SOME NEARBY HOUSES. IT THEN LIFTED JUST BEFORE GREENWELL SPRINGS PORT HUDSON RD.
EM REPORTED TREES DOWN AND DEBRIS NEAR ESSEN LANE AND WESTMINSTER AND I-10. POSSIBLE TORNADO. NO INJURIES.
TREES REPORTED DOWN. POSSIBLE TORNADO.

ALONG THE PATH, MANY TREES WERE DAMAGED. THIS INCLUDED SNAPPED AND UPROOTED TREES AS WELL AS MANY LARGE LIMBS BROKEN. ALONG HIGHWAY 27, MINOR ROOF DAMAGE OCCURRED TO ONE HOME. ALONG MCKENZIE ROAD,MORE SUBSTANTIAL TREE DAMAGE WAS NOTED AND A POWER POLE WAS TAKENDOWN. ANOTHER AREA WHERE MORE SUBSTANTIAL TREE DAMAGE OCCURRED WASSOUTH OF FULGHAM ROAD AND AT THE INTERSECTION OF POSSUM TROT ROAD. IT THEN CROSSED INTO MARION COUNTY. MAX WINDS 95 MPH. RATING EF-1.
TREES AND POWER LINES REPORTED DOWN ON HIGHWAY 27, MCKENZIE ROAD, AND FULGHUM LOOP TO THE SOUTH AND EAST OF SALEM.

REPORTS OF TREE LIMBS DOWN AND TRAFFIC LIGHT NOT WORKING AT HWY 90 AND 15TH STREET. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.

PUBLIC REPORTED BRIEF TORNADO ALONG HWY 90 EAST NEAR COWAN ROAD IN GULFPORT, MS. PRELIMINARY SURVEY INDICATED LARGE TREE BRANCHES CRASHED ONTO A CONDO COMPLEX, CAUSING SOME STRUCTURAL DAMAGE. ESTIMATED WINDS 65 MPH. SEVERAL LARGE HARDWOOD TREE LIMBS SNAPPED AND MINOR ROOF DAMAGE TO SEVERAL HOMES FROM U.S. HIGHWAY 90 NEAR THE AQUARIUM TO THE CSX RAILWAY NEAR HENDERSON AVENUE.
REPORT OF A LIGHT POLE DOWN ON HWY 90 IN GULFPORT. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.

NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND A SHORT TRACK OF DAMAGE WAS NOTED IN THE AREA OF BIG JOHN ROAD AND KROHN LANE. MOST NOTABLE WAS A LARGE HARDWOOD TREE UPROOTED ALONG WITH SEVERAL PINE TREES TWISTED AND SNAPPED. PRELIMINARY RATING OF EF-1. MAX WINDS 100 MPH.

EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTED POWER OUTAGES AND TREE DAAMAGE NEAR 26245 HWY 15 NORTH OF DIBERVILLE. TREES AND POWER LINES WERE ALSO DAMAGED NEAR BIG JOHN ROAD AND 6160 KROHN RD HAD A TREE ON THE ROOF OF A HOUSE REPORTED. POWER LINES WERE REPORTED DOWN ON THE GROUND ON HWY 15 AS WELL.

PUBLIC REPORTED BRIEF TORNADO ALONG HWY 90 EAST NEAR COWAN ROAD IN GULFPORT, MS. PRELIMINARY SURVEY INDICATED LARGE TREE BRANCHES CRASHED ONTO A CONDO COMPLEX, CAUSING SOME STRUCTURAL DAMAGE. ESTIMATED WINDS 60 MPH. SURVEY MAY BE RESUMED FARTHER NORTH WHERE OTHER POINTS OF DAMAGE WERE NOTED NEAR BIG JOHN ROAD AND POSSIBLY ALONG HIGHWAY 15 NORTH OF DIBERVILLE.
PUBLIC REPORTED BRIEF TORNADO ALONG HWY 90 EAST NEAR COWAN ROAD IN GULFPORT, MS.

NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND EF-1 TORNADO DAMAGE ON OLD HWY 15 NORTH OF BILOXI, MS. SMALL POPULATED COMMUNITY ALONG OLD HWY 15 HAD A COUPLE OF HOMES WITH ROOF DAMAGE. MANY TREES WERE VIOLENTLY TWISTED. A POWER POLEWAS SNAPPED AT THE BASE. MAX WINDS 105 MPH.
